introSection: SheiKra is a steel roller coaster located at Busch Gardens Tampa in Tampa, Florida, USA. Built by Bolliger & Mabillard, it was the first Dive Coaster in North America as well as world's tallest and fastest ride of its kind. In 2007, two years after it opened, SheiKra was converted into a floorless roller coaster.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
In October 2004, it was announced that SheiKra would be added to the park. Pieces of track arrived that fall, with vertical construction beginning shortly afterwards.

The ride was topped off in January 2005. Testing then began in April.

SheiKra opened on May 21, 2005. It was the first Dive Coaster in North America.

On May 28, 2007, SheiKra was closed to modify the trains. Just two weeks later on June 16, the ride reopened with the new floorless ride experience.

The attraction was temporarily closed in early 2013 for a repaint.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: SheiKra has a red track and blue supports.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: A car from SheiKra was on display at B&M's booth at the 2005 IAAPA Expo in Atlanta, Georgia, USA.

sections.title: Name origin
sections.content: The name "SheiKra" is derived from a Shikra, a hawk that is known to dive straight down for its prey.

fullText:
SheiKra is a steel roller coaster located at Busch Gardens Tampa in Tampa, Florida, USA. Built by Bolliger & Mabillard, it was the first Dive Coaster in North America as well as world's tallest and fastest ride of its kind. In 2007, two years after it opened, SheiKra was converted into a floorless roller coaster.

In October 2004, it was announced that SheiKra would be added to the park. Pieces of track arrived that fall, with vertical construction beginning shortly afterwards.

The ride was topped off in January 2005. Testing then began in April.

SheiKra opened on May 21, 2005. It was the first Dive Coaster in North America.

On May 28, 2007, SheiKra was closed to modify the trains. Just two weeks later on June 16, the ride reopened with the new floorless ride experience.

The attraction was temporarily closed in early 2013 for a repaint.



SheiKra has a red track and blue supports.

A car from SheiKra was on display at B&M's booth at the 2005 IAAPA Expo in Atlanta, Georgia, USA.

The name "SheiKra" is derived from a Shikra, a hawk that is known to dive straight down for its prey.
